frame_version: v3 -- three confirmed extraction bugs fixed (supersedes v2, commits 4bebfd7/c29e534)
frame_status_note: v2 was ratified 2026-07-04 on data later found to contain 3 confirmed mechanical-extraction bugs, discovered while preparing the GG-approved trial batch (see bug_fixes_this_version below). All 3 are FIXED in agentgauge/exp1_mirror.py and agentgauge/exp1_tool_def_extractor.py, with regression tests. This frame reflects the corrected, re-verified doc-density scores. v2's ratification of the TIERING METHODOLOGY (empirical terciles, exclude confirmed extraction failures) still stands -- only the underlying per-server DATA changed. Re-escalated for ratification because the correction is material: N changes (23->22 fresh), 3 servers change tier, and the headline near-empty-prevalence finding strengthens (2/23 -> 0/22 absolutely near-empty). Do NOT begin contested-task authoring or Arm A/B trials against this frame until GG ratifies.

bug_fixes_this_version.summary: _has_tool_decorator matched ANY decorator containing the substring 'tool', including the low-level MCP SDK's own @server.list_tools()/@server.call_tool() protocol-handler registration methods (required boilerplate on every low-level-SDK server). vitali87-code-graph-rag's real ~10-tool catalog lives behind a dynamic registry (tools.get_tool_schemas()) that is not AST-extractable at all -- correctly excluded now (extraction_failed), not scored as 2 fake near-empty tools.

The fully generic Python regex fallback matched ANY name/description proximity pair, including mcp.types.Prompt(...)/PromptArgument(...) definitions (a DIFFERENT MCP primitive) in the same files as real tools. 7 prompts + their arguments were extracted as 14 phantom tools alongside the 2 real ones found this way. Fixed by adding a precise Tool(name=..., description=...) constructor pattern (tried before the generic fallback) that cannot match Prompt(...). Real count: 8 tools, composite 71.8 -> 100.0 (near_empty -> well_documented).

extract_python_tools (the core, highest-confidence AST path, used by MOST Python servers in this frame) read ONLY the function's own docstring, never a decorator keyword argument like @mcp.tool(name=..., description=...) -- FastMCP's own officially-documented kwarg style. lucasastorian-llmwiki's 13 tools all use this style with substantial real descriptions, but were scored 0% description coverage (composite 25.0). Fixed: decorator kwarg now takes precedence over docstring (matching FastMCP's own runtime behavior); docstring is the fallback when no kwarg is given. Real: 92.3% described, composite=78.1.

blast_radius_verification: Re-ran mirroring + doc-density scoring for ALL 25 servers after each fix to confirm no other server was affected -- confirmed via the AUTHORITATIVE pipeline (scripts/exp1_score_doc_density.py, which correctly parses per-param docstring descriptions). An earlier ad-hoc verification script incorrectly suggested 5 more servers changed; that script had its own bug (it reconstructed ExtractedTool objects from the already-built MirrorTool/MirrorParam objects, which do not carry per-param descriptions, forcing them to '' and understating param_description_coverage). That false alarm was caught and discarded before being reported -- the authoritative re-run confirms EXACTLY the 3 servers listed above changed, no others.
predecessor_frame_note: v2 (commits 4bebfd7, c29e534) superseded -- kept for audit trail.
discovery_source: GitHub topic:mcp-server, 500 repos fetched (unchanged from v1/v2 -- same 30 structurally-vetted candidates, re-scored not re-discovered)
vetting_method_note: Each candidate was screened by an independent research pass against two criteria GG specified: (a) is_focused_mcp_server -- primary purpose is exposing a FIXED MCP tool catalog, not a general platform/framework/aggregator/gateway/SDK-for-building-servers with MCP as an incidental feature; (b) approx_tool_count >= 5, so a confusable family (2+ similarly-named/purposed tools) can plausibly exist. ~55 candidates were screened across the 500-repo discovery pool; 30 passed. A previously-unanticipated failure mode was found and excluded throughout: aggregators/gateways/proxies that dynamically route to OTHER MCP servers rather than exposing their own fixed catalog (e.g. metatool-ai/metamcp, tadata-org/fastapi_mcp) -- these have no stable docstring surface to mirror.

doc_density_methodology.scorer: agentgauge/exp1_doc_density.py compute_doc_density() -- 30% description length, 25% description presence, 25% non-echo descriptions, 20% param-description coverage
doc_density_methodology.extractor: agentgauge/exp1_tool_def_extractor.py -- deterministic, no LLM/agent: git clone --depth 1, static parsing (Python AST decorator + imperative add_tool() + JSON-literal fallback; regex proximity for other languages)
doc_density_methodology.tiering: assign_tiers() -- empirical terciles (p33/p67) of composite_score WITHIN the 23 successfully-scored fresh candidates. Tier names (well_documented/thin/near_empty) are RELATIVE to this sample, not absolute doc-quality bands -- see honest_caveat below.

ratification_2026_07_04_v2: N=23 fresh + 2 anchors ratified; empirical-tercile tiering kept as pre-committed. SUPERSEDED by this v3 correction -- see bug_fixes_this_version.

open_decisions_applied_by_default.issue: After the 3 bugfixes, composite scores across the 22 scoreable fresh servers range 37.3-100.0. 0 of 22 have 0% description coverage (down from 2/23 in v2) and 1 have <20% coverage (oraios-serena, 11.1%, composite=37.3, is now the single closest-to-thin candidate in the entire scoreable pool). The near_empty TERCILE still exists by construction (terciles always produce 3 groups), but its members (37.3-80.1 composite, up to 92%+ description coverage) are not absolutely thin -- e.g. lucasastorian-llmwiki (78.1, 92.3% described) was the server GG approved as the batch-1 trial target BEFORE this correction, on the false premise that it was 0% documented.
open_decisions_applied_by_default.default_applied: None -- explicitly re-escalated, not resolved by default.
open_decisions_applied_by_default.reportable_finding: STRENGTHENS the v2 finding: under-documented public MCP servers are not just rare (2/23) but, once measurement bugs are fixed, nearly ABSENT (0/22 absolutely near-empty) in this GitHub-discoverable, structurally-valid population. This is now a headline-strength result for the paper's regime map: the public segment is overwhelmingly OUT-OF-REGIME on doc-density grounds alone, before any behavioral testing. Register this as THE finding, not a sampling shortfall -- per the frozen protocol's own honest_caveat, this remains a LOWER BOUND on prevalence in the unsampleable under-documented internal segment.

next_step: Awaiting GG ratification of this corrected frame (v3) before any mirroring beyond what already exists, contested-task authoring, or Arm A/B trials. The GG-approved batch-1 trial target (lucasastorian-llmwiki) needs to be reconsidered given its premise (0% documented) is no longer true -- oraios-serena is now the closest candidate to genuinely thin in the corrected pool.
